title: User Input
summary: Validate external text before calculating with it.
refresher: Browser `prompt(...)` returns a string or `null` when canceled. Handle cancellation, trim when appropriate, convert numeric text, and validate the result.
refresher_code:
const raw = prompt("Credits?");
if (raw !== null) console.log(Number(raw));

blocks.body: Browser `prompt(...)` returns a string or `null` when canceled. Handle cancellation, trim when appropriate, convert numeric text, and validate the result. Validate external text before calculating with it.

Input arrives from outside the assumptions in your code. Treat reading, checking, converting, and using as four separate actions. A canceled prompt is `null`, an empty response is `""`, and invalid numeric text becomes `NaN`. Those cases may need different messages.

blocks.caption: The branches separate cancellation from failed number conversion.
Protect the conversion path from cancellation and invalid text.

blocks.body: Follow the corrected path with input `"12"`: prompt returns text, the null check passes, Number produces twelve, validation passes, and addition produces thirteen. With Cancel, the conversion and calculation are skipped.

blocks.title: Check the boundary
blocks.body: Typing 12 into a prompt produces `"12"`; adding one without conversion joins text into `"121"`.

blocks.why: The browser returns null for cancellation, which differs from an entered empty string.
Number performs explicit conversion, while adding zero concatenates text.
